reasoning: Tnfaip3 encodes the ubiquitin‑editing protein A20, a key negative regulator of NF‑κB and other inflammatory signaling pathways. CRISPRi‑mediated knockdown of Tnfaip3 removes this brake, resulting in heightened NF‑κB activity and increased production of pro‑inflammatory cytokines such as TNF‑α and IL‑1β. Lipa (lysosomal acid lipase) is primarily involved in lipid catabolism and is transcriptionally regulated by lipid‑sensing nuclear receptors (e.g., LXR, PPAR) rather than by canonical NF‑κB signaling. There is no well‑established direct link whereby enhanced NF‑κB activity up‑ or down‑regulates Lipa expression. Consequently, loss of A20 is unlikely to cause a consistent change in Lipa transcript levels.
